title: Phyllanthus jarawae
taxonomy: Phyllanthus jarawae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. The genus Phyllanthus is a large and diverse group, with over 1,000 species worldwide. Phyllanthus jarawae is native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, a union territory of India. The plant is considered rare and endangered due to habitat loss and other human activities. It is often associated with the islands' tropical rainforests, where it grows as a shrub or small tree.

intro: Phyllanthus jarawae is a rare and endemic plant species native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been found to exhibit various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, which may help in reducing liver damage and improving overall liver function. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus jarawae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Phyllanthus jarawae include decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are often used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat liver disorders and other health conditions.
